raw_prolog:
:- use_module(library(clpfd)).
solve :-
    Vars = [Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, Q5, Q6, Q7, Q8],
    Vars ins 1..8,
    all_different(Vars),
    
    %% Clue 1: No two queens can be in the same row.
    %% This is already ensured by all_different/1.

    %% Clue 2: No two queens can be in the same column.
    %% This is also already ensured by all_different/1.

    %% Clue 3: No two queens can be on the same diagonal.
    Q1 #\= Q2 + 1, Q1 #\= Q2 - 1,
    Q1 #\= Q3 + 2, Q1 #\= Q3 - 2,
    Q1 #\= Q4 + 3, Q1 #\= Q4 - 3,
    Q1 #\= Q5 + 4, Q1 #\= Q5 - 4,
    Q1 #\= Q6 + 5, Q1 #\= Q6 - 5,
    Q1 #\= Q7 + 6, Q1 #\= Q7 - 6,
    Q1 #\= Q8 + 7, Q1 #\= Q8 - 7,
    
    Q2 #\= Q3 + 1, Q2 #\= Q3 - 1,
    Q2 #\= Q4 + 2, Q2 #\= Q4 - 2,
    Q2 #\= Q5 + 3, Q2 #\= Q5 - 3,
    Q2 #\= Q6 + 4, Q2 #\= Q6 - 4,
    Q2 #\= Q7 + 5, Q2 #\= Q7 - 5,
    Q2 #\= Q8 + 6, Q2 #\= Q8 - 6,

    Q3 #\= Q4 + 1, Q3 #\= Q4 - 1,
    Q3 #\= Q5 + 2, Q3 #\= Q5 - 2,
    Q3 #\= Q6 + 3, Q3 #\= Q6 - 3,
    Q3 #\= Q7 + 4, Q3 #\= Q7 - 4,
    Q3 #\= Q8 + 5, Q3 #\= Q8 - 5,

    Q4 #\= Q5 + 1, Q4 #\= Q5 - 1,
    Q4 #\= Q6 + 2, Q4 #\= Q6 - 2,
    Q4 #\= Q7 + 3, Q4 #\= Q7 - 3,
    Q4 #\= Q8 + 4, Q4 #\= Q8 - 4,

    Q5 #\= Q6 + 1, Q5 #\= Q6 - 1,
    Q5 #\= Q7 + 2, Q5 #\= Q7 - 2,
    Q5 #\= Q8 + 3, Q5 #\= Q8 - 3,

    Q6 #\= Q7 + 1, Q6 #\= Q7 - 1,
    Q6 #\= Q8 + 2, Q6 #\= Q8 - 2,

    Q7 #\= Q8 + 1, Q7 #\= Q8 - 1,

    labeling([], Vars),
    write(Vars), nl.

:- use_module(library(clpfd)).
solve :-
    Vars = [C, C_Sharp, D, D_Sharp, E, F, F_Sharp, G, G_Sharp, A, A_Sharp, B],
    Vars ins 0..11,
    Notes = [C, C_Sharp, D, D_Sharp, E, F, F_Sharp, G, G_Sharp, A, A_Sharp, B],
    all_different(Notes),
    
    %% Clue 1: The interval between the first and second note is a minor second (1).
    abs(C - C_Sharp) #= 1,
    
    %% Clue 2: The interval between the second and third note is a major second (2).
    abs(C_Sharp - D) #= 2,
    
    %% Clue 3: The interval between the third and fourth note is a minor third (3).
    abs(D - D_Sharp) #= 3,
    
    %% Clue 4: The interval between the fourth and fifth note is a major third (4).
    abs(D_Sharp - E) #= 4,
    
    %% Clue 5: The interval between the fifth and sixth note is a perfect fourth (5).
    abs(E - F) #= 5,
    
    %% Clue 6: The interval between the sixth and seventh note is a tritone (6).
    abs(F - F_Sharp) #= 6,
    
    %% Clue 7: The interval between the seventh and eighth note is a perfect fifth (7).
    abs(F_Sharp - G) #= 7,
    
    %% Clue 8: The interval between the eighth and ninth note is a minor sixth (8).
    abs(G - G_Sharp) #= 8,
    
    %% Clue 9: The interval between the ninth and tenth note is a major sixth (9).
    abs(G_Sharp - A) #= 9,
    
    %% Clue 10: The interval between the tenth and eleventh note is a minor seventh (10).
    abs(A - A_Sharp) #= 10,
    
    %% Clue 11: The interval between the eleventh and twelfth note is a major seventh (11).
    abs(A_Sharp - B) #= 11,

    labeling([], Vars),
    write(Vars), nl.

:- use_module(library(clpfd)).
solve :-
    Vars = [Row1_1, Row1_2, Row1_3, Row1_4, Row1_5,
            Row2_1, Row2_2, Row2_3, Row2_4, Row2_5,
            Row3_1, Row3_2, Row3_3, Row3_4, Row3_5,
            Row4_1, Row4_2, Row4_3, Row4_4, Row4_5,
            Row5_1, Row5_2, Row5_3, Row5_4, Row5_5,
            Col1_1, Col1_2, Col1_3, Col1_4, Col1_5,
            Col2_1, Col2_2, Col2_3, Col2_4, Col2_5,
            Col3_1, Col3_2, Col3_3, Col3_4, Col3_5,
            Col4_1, Col4_2, Col4_3, Col4_4, Col4_5,
            Col5_1, Col5_2, Col5_3, Col5_4, Col5_5],
    Vars ins 0..1,
    Rows = [Row1_1, Row1_2, Row1_3, Row1_4, Row1_5,
             Row2_1, Row2_2, Row2_3, Row2_4, Row2_5,
             Row3_1, Row3_2, Row3_3, Row3_4, Row3_5,
             Row4_1, Row4_2, Row4_3, Row4_4, Row4_5,
             Row5_1, Row5_2, Row5_3, Row5_4, Row5_5],
    Columns = [Col1_1, Col1_2, Col1_3, Col1_4, Col1_5,
               Col2_1, Col2_2, Col2_3, Col2_4, Col2_5,
               Col3_1, Col3_2, Col3_3, Col3_4, Col3_5,
               Col4_1, Col4_2, Col4_3, Col4_4, Col4_5,
               Col5_1, Col5_2, Col5_3, Col5_4, Col5_5],
    all_different(Rows),
    all_different(Columns),

    %% Clue 1: The first row has a block of 3 shaded squares.
    Row1_1 #= 1, Row1_2 #= 1, Row1_3 #= 1, Row1_4 #= 0, Row1_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 2: The second row has a block of 2 shaded squares.
    Row2_1 #= 0, Row2_2 #= 1, Row2_3 #= 1, Row2_4 #= 0, Row2_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 3: The third row has a block of 1 shaded square.
    Row3_1 #= 0, Row3_2 #= 0, Row3_3 #= 1, Row3_4 #= 0, Row3_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 4: The fourth row has a block of 4 shaded squares.
    Row4_1 #= 1, Row4_2 #= 1, Row4_3 #= 1, Row4_4 #= 1, Row4_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 5: The fifth row has a block of 5 shaded squares.
    Row5_1 #= 1, Row5_2 #= 1, Row5_3 #= 1, Row5_4 #= 1, Row5_5 #= 1,

    %% Clue 6: The first column has a block of 2 shaded squares.
    Col1_1 #= 0, Col1_2 #= 1, Col1_3 #= 1, Col1_4 #= 0, Col1_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 7: The second column has a block of 3 shaded squares.
    Col2_1 #= 1, Col2_2 #= 1, Col2_3 #= 1, Col2_4 #= 0, Col2_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 8: The third column has a block of 1 shaded square.
    Col3_1 #= 0, Col3_2 #= 0, Col3_3 #= 1, Col3_4 #= 0, Col3_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 9: The fourth column has a block of 2 shaded squares.
    Col4_1 #= 0, Col4_2 #= 1, Col4_3 #= 1, Col4_4 #= 0, Col4_5 #= 0,

    %% Clue 10: The fifth column has a block of 1 shaded square.
    Col5_1 #= 0, Col5_2 #= 0, Col5_3 #= 0, Col5_4 #= 1, Col5_5 #= 0,

    labeling([], Vars),
    write(Vars), nl.

:- use_module(library(clpfd)).
solve :-
    Vars = [Battleship, Cruiser1, Cruiser2, Destroyer1, Destroyer2, Destroyer3, Submarine1, Submarine2, Submarine3, Submarine4],
    Vars ins 1..10,
    ShipTypes = [Battleship, Cruiser1, Cruiser2, Destroyer1, Destroyer2, Destroyer3, Submarine1, Submarine2, Submarine3, Submarine4],
    all_different(ShipTypes),
    
    %% Clue 1: The battleship is in the first row.
    Battleship #= 1,
    
    %% Clue 2: The cruiser is in the second row.
    Cruiser1 #= 2,
    Cruiser2 #= 3,
    
    %% Clue 3: The destroyer is in the third row.
    Destroyer1 #= 4,
    Destroyer2 #= 5,
    Destroyer3 #= 6,
    
    %% Clue 4: The submarine is in the fourth row.
    Submarine1 #= 7,
    Submarine2 #= 8,
    Submarine3 #= 9,
    Submarine4 #= 10,
    
    labeling([], Vars),
    write(Vars), nl.

:- use_module(library(clpfd)).
solve :-
    Vars = [A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, N, O, P, Q, R, S],
    Vars ins 1..19,
    all_different(Vars),
    
    %% Clue 1: Diagonal A+B+C = 38
    A + B + C #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 2: Diagonal D+E+F+G = 38
    D + E + F + G #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 3: Diagonal H+I+J+K+L = 38
    H + I + J + K + L #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 4: Diagonal M+N+O+P = 38
    M + N + O + P #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 5: Diagonal Q+R+S = 38
    Q + R + S #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 6: Diagonal A+D+H = 38
    A + D + H #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 7: Diagonal B+E+I+M = 38
    B + E + I + M #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 8: Diagonal C+F+J+N+Q = 38
    C + F + J + N + Q #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 9: Diagonal G+K+O+R = 38
    G + K + O + R #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 10: Diagonal L+P+S = 38
    L + P + S #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 11: Diagonal C+G+L = 38
    C + G + L #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 12: Diagonal B+F+K+P = 38
    B + F + K + P #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 13: Diagonal A+E+J+O+S = 38
    A + E + J + O + S #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 14: Diagonal D+I+N+R = 38
    D + I + N + R #= 38,
    
    %% Clue 15: Diagonal H+M+Q = 38
    H + M + Q #= 38,

    labeling([], Vars),
    write(Vars), nl.
